briefing.opening: Good morning. I will now begin the climate briefing for March 28, 2026.

sections.title: 🌱 Innovative Responses of Agriculture and Fisheries for Climate Change Adaptation
sections.content: Due to the impacts of climate change, proactive and scientific adaptation efforts are actively underway in Korea's agricultural and fisheries sectors. Scientific farming is being promoted in response to changes in crop cultivation environments, such as ginseng cultivation areas moving northward and settling in Hongcheon [1]. In particular, fisheries research institutions nationwide are gathering in Yeosu, Jeollanam-do, to hold a seminar to strengthen cooperation in responding to climate change [3, 6], focusing on joint research and seeking countermeasures for changes in marine ecosystems. Jeonbuk Self-Governing Province also finalized its 2026 Agricultural and Rural Climate Change Response Implementation Plan, presenting a concrete roadmap for accelerating carbon neutrality [5]. These efforts are crucial steps to ensure the sustainability of the domestic agricultural and fisheries industries in the face of new challenges posed by climate change.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Accelerating Domestic Efforts to Achieve a Carbon-Neutral Society
sections.content: Various local governments and institutions across Korea are making multifaceted efforts to realize a carbon-neutral society. Uijeongbu City is contributing to air quality improvement and carbon neutrality goals through the dissemination of electric motorcycles [7], while Hwaseong City and Yeungnam University are planting the seeds of a future carbon-neutral society with tree-planting campaigns [8, 12]. Gwangju Seo-gu is focusing on raising citizen awareness by operating customized carbon-neutral environmental education for all ages, from infants to adults [9, 10], and Incheon Dong-gu plans to spread a culture of practical action through its 'Visiting Environmental Classroom' [16]. In terms of technological innovation, ETRI has developed an AI-based factory energy management system to support the acceleration of carbon neutrality in manufacturing sites [13], and the approval of the hydrogen-reduced steel site in Pohang is expected to be a significant turning point for the carbon-neutral transition in the industrial sector [14]. As such, comprehensive efforts are actively underway, similar to the activities of the '2045 Carbon Neutrality and Green Growth Realization Special Committee' of the Chungnam Provincial Council [15].

sections.title: 🌱 Progress and Challenges in Renewable Energy Transition ⚠️
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is actively being pursued both domestically and internationally, but challenges are also emerging in the process. Gunsan City is promoting a transition to an advanced industrial city based on renewable energy [17, 19], and Haenam County is creating a 'renewable energy self-sufficient city' through public-private cooperation [18, 21], demonstrating active efforts at the local government level. Gwangju City and Daegu City are accelerating the energy transition by supporting the installation costs of new and renewable energy in homes [22, 26], and positive movements are spreading, such as the Daegu Regional Environmental Office holding a regional climate and energy council [24]. The analysis that the cause of the major power outage in Spain was not due to renewable energy makes a significant contribution to dispelling misunderstandings about the stability of renewable energy [25]. However, the case of the UK blocking a Chinese wind power project due to national security concerns suggests international political conflicts in the renewable energy sector [23], and the Yeongdeok wind power plant fire raises awareness about safety management in renewable energy facility operations and the issue of 'outsourcing risk' [27].

sections.title: 🌍 International Impacts of Climate Change and Call to Action
sections.content: Climate change is a global issue, with its impacts extending to international power dynamics, and the international community's attention and call for action continue. Arthur Snell's book 'Elemental' analyzes how climate change affects who holds global power, emphasizing the importance of this issue [29]. Meanwhile, the voices of local residents directly affected by climate change are growing louder, with small Alaskan communities holding rallies at the Capitol to urge climate action [28]. Reports that India can also tell a positive story regarding climate change [30] suggest that each country is responding to climate change in its own way, once again highlighting the importance of international cooperation and solidarity.

briefing.closing: As we have seen in today's briefing, responding to climate change is now a process of grand transition unfolding across all aspects of our lives. Amidst various domestic and international efforts and challenges, wise choices and actions for a sustainable future will become even more crucial. This concludes today's climate briefing for March 28, 2026.
